issues.title: Private key material exposed through constructor and getEncoded()
issues.problem: The constructor stores the caller's byte array reference directly instead of cloning, and getEncoded() returns the internal keyBytes array without cloning. This allows external code to modify the private key material, breaking key immutability and creating a security vulnerability.

issues.title: Native library extracted to predictable shared temp path without restrictive permissions
issues.problem: The native library is always extracted to a predictable path (/tmp/sdf4j-jce-native/<platform>-libsdf4j-jce.so) and then loaded. On multi-user systems, another user could potentially replace the library before it's loaded. Additionally, the file permissions are not set restrictively after extraction.

issues.title: SM4Context struct missing key_handle field causes resource leak
issues.problem: The SM4Context structure does not store the imported key handle. When streaming operations call SDF_ImportKey in sm4EncryptInit/sm4DecryptInit, the returned key handle is passed to SDF_EncryptInit/DecryptInit but never stored. The sm4Free function never calls SDF_DestroyKey, causing key handle leaks on the SDF device.

issues.title: Decrypt init reads IV buffer without length validation
issues.problem: The sm4DecryptInit function calls GetByteArrayElements on the IV array without first validating its length. Unlike sm4EncryptInit which checks IV length at lines 322-327, sm4DecryptInit directly accesses the IV pointer, potentially reading past the end of a short IV array.

issues.title: Imported SM4 key handles never destroyed in streaming operations
issues.problem: After successfully importing a key via SDF_ImportKey, the key handle is not stored in the context. The sm4Free function only clears and frees the context memory without calling SDF_DestroyKey, causing cumulative resource leaks on the SDF device.

issues.title: Public key coordinates exposed without cloning
issues.problem: The getX() and getY() methods return the internal x and y byte arrays directly without cloning. This allows external code to modify the public key coordinates, which could break cryptographic operations.

issues.title: DER parser silently truncates oversized signature components and accepts trailing garbage
issues.problem: The copyToFixedBuffer function silently truncates data if srcLen > 32 bytes. Additionally, derToRaw() only checks that at least the declared SEQUENCE length remains, so trailing garbage in the DER input is accepted. This means malformed DER signatures can be transformed into different (r,s) pairs instead of being rejected.

issues.title: Cipher context not implicitly reset after doFinal violates JCE spec
issues.problem: The JCE Cipher specification requires the cipher to be implicitly reset after doFinal. Since doFinalStreaming sets ctx=0, a subsequent update() or doFinal() call incorrectly throws IllegalStateException. The cipher should lazily re-initialize the context for subsequent operations.

issues.title: engineGetOutputSize ignores buffered data violating CipherSpi contract
issues.problem: According to the CipherSpi contract, engineGetOutputSize must account for any data currently held in the internal buffer. Failing to include buffer.size() can cause the caller to allocate too small an output array, resulting in ShortBufferException.

issues.title: engineGetOutputSize ignores buffered data
issues.problem: SM2Cipher's engineGetOutputSize doesn't account for data in the buffer, violating the CipherSpi contract and potentially causing ShortBufferException.

issues.title: SDFProvider constructor ignores libraryPath parameter
issues.problem: The SDFProvider(String libraryPath) constructor accepts a libraryPath parameter but delegates to the default constructor which ignores it entirely. The parameter is documented as ignored, but this breaks the API contract and provider-only use cases.

issues.title: GCM tag length from GCMParameterSpec accepted but not enforced
issues.problem: The cipher accepts any GCMParameterSpec tag length and uses gcmTagLenBits to size buffers and split the tag on decrypt, but the native sm4AuthEnc/sm4AuthDec functions always generate and expect a fixed 16-byte tag. Requests for non-128-bit tags will produce incorrect getOutputSize() calculations and broken authentication.

issues.title: Resource cleanup relies only on finalize() which is unreliable
issues.problem: SM4Cipher only cleans up the sessionHandle in finalize(), which is not guaranteed to run. This pattern is repeated across multiple JCE classes (SM2Signature, SM3MessageDigest, SDFSecureRandom, SM2KeyPairGenerator, SM4KeyGenerator, HmacSM3, SM2Cipher, SM4Mac), creating potential resource leaks.

issues.title: Race condition in initialization check
issues.problem: The g_sdf_initialized flag is checked without mutex protection. Between the check and the set, another thread could see an inconsistent state. While unlikely in practice, this could cause issues in multi-threaded initialization scenarios.

issues.title: Incorrect exception type for empty plaintext
issues.problem: When plainLen is 0, the code throws OutOfMemoryError instead of IllegalArgumentException. This is misleading for developers trying to debug the issue.
